essence: David scopes a 'clotting lab event' skill that drives Playwright MCP against his david@ideasmen.com.au calendar to scrape the March 12 event and document the steps so the recurring Thursday-night Clauding Lab event can be created autonomously (triggers: 4C event, clotting lab, Thursday night), while also asking to add a repo to upstreams and hunt for a lost sites/SiteSinner folder on the MacBook Pro.

actions.text: Create the 'clotting lab event' skill: navigate the calendar site, document event-creation steps, set up as a recurring event
Add the referenced repository to upstreams

essence: Evening call with Mary and Jan settles tomorrow's plan: run the vOz meeting (capped ~2 hours so they can still pick up the computer, then take the day off), ask Foz at the start whether to build a new agent or continue the kid-podcast project, with Mary proposing a story-analyzer agent because Byron cannot analyze stories for the boy Peter; David will hand over via Loom transcript so they continue independently.

actions.text: Ask Foz on tomorrow's call whether to prioritize a new agent or the kid-podcast project, then hand over via Loom transcript

loop.label: vOz agent direction (new agent vs kid podcast)

essence: David runs the Clauding Lab live demo at 4C on a just-rebuilt Mac: builds an appydave.com replacement with Claude Code (brains context, focus skill, Ralphie requirements-plan-build loop), spins four marketing-angle site variants in parallel git worktrees via background agents (renders failed), teaches the four multi-agent 'room' techniques (crowded room, handover, background agents, swarm), pitches the Samantha personal-agent/agentic-OS vision and 5-machine fleet, plugs appydave/AITLDR/Clauding Lab channels, then voice-notes Jan asking to move the Voss meeting to Saturday morning because the Power Mac arrives tomorrow.

actions.text: Call Jan: Power Mac arrives tomorrow — can the Voss meeting move to Saturday morning; take the day off otherwise

essence: David and Chris align on AngelEye ('Angel I') telemetry architecture: per-source transformers normalize hook streams and detailed JSON exports into one unified schema (extras kept as metadata), 'workspaces' group related sessions, raw stream logging stays separate from ETL cleanup, and file-system storage beats Supabase/SQLite for the agile v0 despite known scaling limits; separately David meets Charlotte, 4C's new community manager, about recurring Clauding Lab room bookings and upcoming organizer docs.

actions.text: Send Chris the AngelEye v0-alpha repo and schema once built tonight; compare against his DB schema
Book the 4C room as a recurring weekly event for Clauding Lab sessions

essence: David orders the flaky test skipped with a documented reason (test.skip), the changes committed and pushed, and the data directory restored clean — the churn he'd asked to be fixed earlier still wasn't done.

actions.text: Skip the flaky test with a reason, commit and push, and clean the churning data directory

essence: David approves smarter test polling on condition it is test-side only and generic/reusable across many tests, then wants the suite rerun with a loop giving human-visible progress plus a measured old-vs-new speed comparison, with test quality checked throughout.

actions.text: Implement generic smarter polling, rerun the suite with a progress loop, and measure new-vs-old speed

essence: David triages three e2e failures — dig into tests 12 and 13 — and orders git restore on the data directory and the other spec files to clear repository churn, noting his local folder is still messy after the restore was supposedly done.

actions.text: Investigate failing tests 12 and 13; git restore the data directory and other spec files to clear churn

essence: Marathon session with two strands: AngelEye design lands on sessions arriving in an unsorted inbox then assigned (manually or auto-learned from folder/patterns) into named workspaces, multi-format ingestion (hooks + JSON exports) documented as a requirements doc in the apps folder; and resyncing the repaired MacBook Pro from the M4 Mini source-of-truth — Ansible discovery, Tailscale personal-plan (3 users/100 devices) for the future Jan+Mary Philippines fleet, locations.json gap analysis on both machines, folder migrations decided case-by-case (move deckhand/thumbrack/labs/legacy SupportSignal, copy dev-historical, delete dead gems, don't touch AWB Dropbox or video projects), public GitHub remotes for DeckHand/AngelEye/AppyStack, global skills synced to the MBP, and tonight's library-meetup agenda committed into the AngelEye repo.

actions.text: Write AngelEye detailed requirements (multi-format ingestion, workspaces/inbox model, future pattern intelligence) in the apps folder alongside DeckHand/ThumbRack/AppyStack
Complete MBP↔M4 Mini sync: locations.json gap analysis, agreed folder moves/copies/deletes, global skills sync; M4 Mini is source of truth
Create public GitHub remotes for DeckHand and AngelEye
Commit tonight's library-meeting agenda file into the AngelEye repo and push

essence: Frustrated design review of the observability app: rename it 'Angel I', drop Supabase and rethink storage toward JSONL/file-system (AppyStack's core strength), clarify E2E vs UAT execution with per-test logging plus a loop monitor for progress, ask how hook scripts get packaged/delivered via the plugins directory, demand an ASCII mockup and written spec — David is angry that background work runs invisibly with no logging and fears his system is being damaged; ends asking how the MBP→M4 Mini file migration was done for a gap analysis now the laptop is back.

actions.text: Write up the E2E logging/feedback design and an ASCII interface mockup in-conversation
Rename the application to Angel I and redesign storage away from Supabase toward file-system/JSONL

essence: Reorientation check on the app build at 7% context remaining: David asks for the overall build overview and whether a handover to another window is due, confirms he likes the app's name, and states the governing principle — the goal is a working application, not unit-test perfectionism.

David demos to Angela and Ronnie two new Claude Code quality-of-life features surfaced by his daily Anthropic release-notes monitor: /loop (a session-scoped cron giving minute-by-minute status reports on his background e2e run, now 185 pass / 4 fail / 11 skip) and /btw (fire a background task mid-conversation without breaking flow); he also flags being unavailable on the 17th and 19th.

David frames his multi-system agent architecture as parallel Chinese factories that assemble on-site — multiple systems running simultaneously because multiple agents work for him — clarifying that 'casino' is not a system but a communication agent letting the factories talk, and stressing that people (like Angela) should quickly flag when something is outside their wheelhouse.
